_comment: Pterodactyl egg for running Blockbench desktop with the blockbench-mcp-plugin through noVNC. Requires a Docker image built from the included docker/Dockerfile. Pterodactyl readiness means noVNC is ready; MCP is ready only after the plugin is loaded inside Blockbench.
name: Blockbench MCP noVNC
author: PastaHimself@users.noreply.github.com
description: Runs Blockbench desktop in a noVNC session and provides the Blockbench MCP plugin file. The MCP endpoint only exists after the plugin is loaded in Blockbench. Default manual MCP settings: port 3000, endpoint /bb-mcp.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
mkdir -p /mnt/server/mcp-plugin /mnt/server/.config/Blockbench /mnt/server/logs
curl -fsSL https://jasonjgardner.github.io/blockbench-mcp-plugin/mcp.js -o /mnt/server/mcp-plugin/mcp.js
cat > /mnt/server/START_HERE.txt <<'TXT'
Blockbench MCP noVNC server installed.

First boot:
1. Open the server web/noVNC allocation in your browser.
2. In Blockbench, use File > Plugins > Load Plugin from File.
3. Select /home/container/mcp-plugin/mcp.js.
4. Allow network access if Blockbench asks.
5. Check Settings > General > MCP Server Port = 3000 and MCP Server Endpoint = /bb-mcp.
6. Pterodactyl marks the server ready when noVNC is available, not when MCP is available.
7. MCP exists only after the plugin is loaded inside Blockbench.
8. Connect your MCP client to http://localhost:3000/bb-mcp from inside the same VPS/container network, or expose the MCP allocation only through VPN/Tailscale/SSH tunnel/authenticated proxy.
TXT
chmod -R u+rwX /mnt/server

variables.name: Expected MCP Port
variables.description: Expected/manual Blockbench MCP port displayed in first-run instructions. This does not automatically configure Blockbench; set the same value in Blockbench Settings > General.

variables.name: Expected MCP Endpoint
variables.description: Expected/manual Blockbench MCP endpoint displayed in first-run instructions. This does not automatically configure Blockbench; set the same value in Blockbench Settings > General.

variables.name: VNC Password
variables.description: Password for VNC/noVNC access. Standard VNC authentication uses exactly 8 characters; change this and keep noVNC behind localhost/VPN/private networking. The startup script writes an x11vnc auth file without passing the password in the long-running x11vnc process arguments.
